medical_history.allergies_line_1: Seasonal allergic rhinitis (pollen, grass) – causes sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, and mild nasal congestion in spring and early fall. Managed with daily antihistamine during peak seasons; no history of anaphylaxis.
medical_history.allergies_line_2: Mild sensitivity to cats – prolonged exposure leads to itchy eyes and mild wheezing; family avoids long visits to homes with multiple cats. No epinephrine auto-injector prescribed.
medical_history.allergies_line_3: No known drug allergies. Has previously taken amoxicillin and azithromycin without adverse reactions.
medical_history.allergies_line_4: No known food allergies. Tolerates peanuts, tree nuts, dairy, eggs, soy, wheat, and seafood without issues.
medical_history.current_prescribed_medications_line_1: Cetirizine 10 mg tablet by mouth once daily during allergy seasons (typically March–May and September–October). Taken at home in the morning; not usually needed during school hours.
medical_history.current_prescribed_medications_line_2: Albuterol HFA inhaler 90 mcg: 2 puffs via spacer every 4–6 hours as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath. Rarely needed; last used during a cold approximately 6 months ago. Inhaler kept at school nurse’s office per asthma action plan.
medical_history.current_prescribed_medications_line_3: Multivitamin chewable once daily with breakfast at home. No other routine prescription medications.
medical_history.significant_historical_information_line_: History of mild intermittent asthma diagnosed at age 6, primarily triggered by upper respiratory infections and seasonal allergies. No hospitalizations or ICU admissions. One emergency room visit at age 7 for wheezing during a viral infection; treated with nebulized albuterol and discharged the same day.

Full-term vaginal birth at 39 weeks with no complications. Birth weight 7 lb 8 oz. Normal developmental milestones: sat without support by 6 months, walked by 13 months, first words around 12 months. No history of NICU stay or congenital conditions reported.

Past injuries/illnesses: left distal radius (wrist) buckle fracture at age 9 after a fall from a playground structure; treated with a short-arm cast for 4 weeks, fully healed with no residual pain or limitation. Occasional tension-type headaches related to dehydration or prolonged screen time; relieved with rest, fluids, and occasional acetaminophen.

Family history: father with hypertension diagnosed in his late 30s and seasonal allergies; maternal grandfather with type 2 diabetes; no family history of early sudden cardiac death, congenital heart disease, seizure disorders, or major psychiatric illness disclosed. No tobacco exposure at home; parents do not smoke and have asked extended family not to smoke around Ethan.

screening_results.hct_hgb: Hemoglobin 13.1 g/dL (normal range for age) – no evidence of anemia.
screening_results.lead: Capillary blood lead <3.3 µg/dL – below level of concern.
screening_results.urinalysis: Screening dipstick normal: negative for glucose, protein, ketones, blood, and nitrites; specific gravity and pH within expected range.
screening_results.gross_dental_abnormal_description: Mild plaque buildup along lower molars and early gingival inflammation noted, likely related to inconsistent flossing. No obvious caries, fractures, or malocclusion visible on gross inspection. Parent reports last dental cleaning was approximately 9 months ago and that a follow-up appointment is already scheduled for routine cleaning and evaluation.
screening_results.gross_dental_refer_tx: Encouraged continuation of regular dental checkups every 6 months. Recommended improved daily oral hygiene: brushing twice daily with fluoride toothpaste for at least 2 minutes and beginning daily flossing, especially around molars. Parent advised to discuss mild gum inflammation with dentist at upcoming appointment.
